latest.title: Accounting date, Results & Suspension -Replacement
latest.content:
30 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2651K
Silver Bullet Data Services Grp PLC
30 June 2026
This announcement replaces the announcement with RNS number: 2528K released on 29 June 2026 at 6:18 p.m., with the headline "Change to Accounting Reference Date, Delay in Publication of Annual Results and Suspension". It is noted that the only change that has been made to the announcement has been to update the prospective date of the last day of trading in the Company's Ordinary Shares from 3 July 2026 to 7 July 2026.
30 June 2026
Silver Bullet Data Services Group plc
("
Silverbullet
" or the "
Company
", or, together with its subsidiaries, the "
Group
")
Change to Accounting Reference Date, Delay in Publication of Annual Results and Suspension
Silverbullet (AIM: SBDS), a provider of AI driven digital transformation services and products, provides the following update with respect to its audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2025 ("Annual Results") and announces a suspension from trading on AIM.
On the 26 June 2026, it resolved to change its accounting reference date and financial year end from 31 December to 30 June. This change takes immediate effect.
As a result of this change in the accounting reference date and assuming continued admission to trading on AIM, the last day of trading for which is 7 July 2026 and which is anticipated to be cancelled at 7:00 a.m. (BST) on 8 July 2026, Silverbullet will adhere to the following reporting timetable:
·
publication of its audited results for the 18-month period to 30 June 2026 by 31 December 2026;
·
publication of its unaudited interim financial results for the 6-month period ending 31 December 2026 by 30 June 2027; and
·
publication of its audited results for the 12-month period to 30 June 2027 by 31 December 2027.
As stipulated by Rule 19 of the AIM Rules for Companies (the "AIM Rules"), the Company is required to publish its Annual Results by 30 June 2026. The Company will not to meet this deadline. Accordingly, trading in the Company's ordinary shares on AIM is expected to be suspended with effect from 7.30 a.m. (BST) on 1 July 2026, pending publication of the Annual Results.
For further information please contact:
Silverbullet
Ian James (CEO) / Chris Ellis (CFO)
via IFC
Strand Hanson Limited - Financial and Nominated Adviser
James Spinney / James Bellman / Imogen Ellis
0207 409 3494
Oberon Capital - Broker
Mike Seabrook
020 3179 5300
IFC Advisory
Graham Herring / Tim Metcalfe / Florence Staton
020 3934 663007793 839 024
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Silver Bullet Data Services Group supplies cloud‑based data‑integration and analytics solutions for mid‑market manufacturers, helping them connect legacy systems to modern SaaS platforms.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a niche where manufacturers still struggle to digitise their operations. Its plug‑and‑play connectors reduce integration costs and speed up time‑to‑value for clients that need to modernise without a full IT overhaul.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a restructuring phase: it has announced accounting changes, suspended a recent earnings release, and is winding down a subsidiary. The stock is currently not actively traded, suggesting liquidity is low and the company may be on the brink of a delisting or bankruptcy filing.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy reshuffling its financial reporting dates and has temporarily suspended its latest results, signalling uncertainty about its cash flow.
A subsidiary, Silver Bullet Data Services Group, has been cancelled, pointing to a strategic exit from a low‑margin product line.
Shareholder meetings have produced mixed outcomes, with a minority of votes supporting a proposed change in governance, indicating internal disagreement about the company’s future direction.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bewildered investors fearing a quick exit
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A single bullish comment suggests that if the company can secure new funding or pivot its product offering, a turnaround is possible, but this view is isolated.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most chatter is negative: shareholders feel misled by board decisions, and there is concern about the company’s liquidity and potential delisting.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from cautious optimism in April to outright pessimism in June, with little sign of renewed enthusiasm.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, meaning the public is largely unaware of any forthcoming news – a classic contrarian signal.

near_term_catalysts: A formal filing to resume trading on a London market or a formal liquidation notice could trigger a final price spike.
Any announcement of new capital injection or a strategic partnership with a larger software vendor would be a clear catalyst for a bounce.
If the company can publish a clear, credible turnaround plan in its next filing, that may restore investor confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden uptick in daily volume that breaks the 10‑day moving average, or a news release that confirms a new funding round or a partnership deal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the very late stage of a crash cycle – liquidity is thin, and the price is stuck in a low‑volume pocket. A mean‑reversion bounce would require a tangible catalyst to break the dead‑zone.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company manages to secure a sizeable capital injection or a strategic partnership, the price could climb to 50% of its pre‑crash level, delivering a 100% upside from the current floor. The asymmetric reward comes from the fact that the downside is capped at the current low.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm is already in a liquidity crisis, has cancelled a subsidiary, and its board has faced shareholder backlash. Without new capital, the risk of delisting or bankruptcy is high. The worst‑case scenario is a liquidation at the current price, meaning no recovery.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: up to 2–3× the current price if a catalyst appears. Potential downside: complete loss of the current position if the company is wound down. The asymmetry is heavily skewed toward loss due to the lack of clear upside drivers.

playbook.if_bullish: If a credible funding announcement appears, enter at the 95th percentile of recent lows, size a small position (e.g., 5% of portfolio), and place a stop 10% below the entry to limit loss to the current floor.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for a volume spike above the 20‑day average coupled with a positive news release. That would be a trigger to consider a short‑term bet.
playbook.if_passing: If a formal liquidation notice is issued or the company fails to resume trading, exit immediately. No position should be held in a firm with imminent delisting.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SBDS.L is in a deep liquidity crisis with no clear catalysts; the risk of loss outweighs the modest upside potential, making a bearish stance the safest contrarian choice.
